Item 2. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

Net sales increased 13 percent year-over-year, with growth across all three segments: North America, International, and Amazon Web Services, reflecting continued strength in our core commerce and cloud businesses.

Amazon Web Services revenue grew 19 percent year-over-year as demand for cloud infrastructure and artificial intelligence services accelerated, driven by enterprise digital transformation and generative AI adoption.

Advertising revenue grew 18 percent year-over-year as brands increased spending on sponsored products and display formats that connect them with customers at the point of purchase intent.

International segment operating losses narrowed meaningfully as newer geographies approached profitability through cost discipline and growing Prime membership density that improves delivery economics.

Capital expenditures increased significantly as we invested in AWS data center capacity, robotics and automation equipment for fulfillment, and last-mile delivery fleet expansion in domestic and international markets.

Item 3. Other Information
